My advice is to never make decisions based on the past, based on sunken cost fallacy. Calculate what is the best decision as if you were born at this very stage and were to choose what is the best decision. I miself believe that this big drop shows that the market understands that there isn't a chance for ceasefires in this small of a time-frame. If the market were rational, even a phone call wouldn't change that in the slightest. However your chance might be if the market were irrational (which it often is) and would buy just for the sake of positive news even tho the case for ceasefires still is basically 0. However, I wouldn't bet on that irrationality being too big, neither on it happening soon enough to warrant possibly losing even more over the days to come in-between now and said call.

very well said. I agree with you. A phone call would just be publicity from both sides and I am hoping for irrationality. Maybe I try to slowly back out and if something happens in early February, I get a bit more.

Possibly, however do calculate and take into account the others losing faith as well and selling much faster and much more than you would have goten out of a phone call that no one knows when will be publicly stated. By then it might drop to 15% or 10% and after that might rise to whoever knows what. If I were you, i wouldnt bet on a exit based on the call between trump and putin since your success would be contingent on 2 things happening at once: it happening very very fast (if not youl lose alot by the time its finally announced), and 2, the irationality of the market being big enough to out weigh how much the market would sell by then.
